title: How to Generate SRT Subtitles for YouTube Videos
deck: YouTube auto-captions look fine until you need a real file. Here’s the creator workflow we actually use to generate SRT subtitles — from link paste to export — without living inside a heavy editor.

ogTitle: How to Generate SRT Subtitles for YouTube Videos in 2026
ogDescription: A practical guide to generating SRT subtitle files for YouTube — tools, tradeoffs, and a workflow that doesn’t break on mobile.

faqSchema.question: What is an SRT subtitle file?
faqSchema.answer: An SRT (SubRip) file is a plain-text subtitle format with numbered cues, start/end timestamps, and caption lines. Video editors and YouTube accept SRT uploads to display timed captions on your video.

faqSchema.question: How do I generate subtitles automatically?
faqSchema.answer: Upload your video or paste a YouTube link into an AI subtitle tool. The service transcribes speech, assigns timestamps, and lets you download an SRT. Review the file before publishing — especially names and technical terms.

faqSchema.question: Can I export subtitles from YouTube?
faqSchema.answer: Sometimes. If YouTube auto-captions exist for your video, Studio may let you download a caption file. Availability varies by channel, language, and whether you own the content. It is not a reliable production workflow.

faqSchema.question: Are AI subtitle generators accurate?
faqSchema.answer: For clear English speech, modern AI tools produce a strong first draft. Accuracy drops with heavy accents, crosstalk, and background music. Always skim the SRT before upload.

faqSchema.question: Which subtitle tool works best on mobile?
faqSchema.answer: Lightweight tools that return a file quickly — without loading a full browser editor — work best on phones. Heavy timeline UIs often lag or fail on mobile browsers.

faqSchema.question: Can I edit SRT files manually?
faqSchema.answer: Yes. SRT is plain text. You can edit in any text editor, Subtitle Edit, or inside your NLE. Fix wording and timing, then re-import to YouTube or your editor.

faqSchema.question: How do I fix SRT timing drift after editing video?
faqSchema.answer: Re-align cues to your final cut: either re-export subtitles from the finished timeline, or shift cue timestamps in your editor. Avoid editing video after subtitles without updating the SRT.

faqSchema.question: Does YouTube accept SRT uploads?
faqSchema.answer: Yes. In YouTube Studio, open Subtitles for a video, upload your SRT, and review the preview. Match the language label to your caption track.
